Company Overview

Lean Transitions Solutions (LTS) is a global Operational Excellence and Lean Industry 4.0 software company headquartered in the UK. For over a decade, we’ve helped large organizations turn strategy into action by combining Lean thinking, leadership principles, and modern, scalable technology. With teams across India, Europe, the US, the Middle East, and Mexico, we operate as one connected global team. At LTS, we believe technology strengthens people, enabling them to do their best work. This belief shapes our products, culture, and how we work together.

Location: Technopark Phase 1, Trivandrum Website: www.LeanTransitionSolutions.com

Role Overview

We are seeking an experienced and motivated Scrum Master to support our Agile teams in delivering high-quality products through effective collaboration and continuous improvement. The ideal candidate will guide teams in adopting Agile best practices, facilitate Scrum ceremonies, and help remove obstacles that impact delivery. This role is key to fostering a transparent, productive, and high-performing Agile environment.

Key Responsibilities

  • Facilitate all Scrum ceremonies: Sprint Scope and Planning, Daily Stand-ups, Sprint Reviews, and Retrospectives.
  • Coach and mentor team members and stakeholders on Agile/Scrum best practices.
  • Track and communicate team velocity, sprint progress, and delivery metrics to stakeholders.
  • Identify and proactively remove blockers and impediments that hinder team progress.
  • Foster a culture of accountability, collaboration, transparency, and continuous improvement.
  • Collaborate with business stakeholders and internal teams to ensure the product backlog or task list is clearly defined, prioritized, and aligned with business goals.
  • Protect the Scrum team from external disruptions and scope changes during active sprints to maintain focus and delivery commitments.
  • Support the organization’s Agile adoption and transformation by promoting Agile values, principles, and best practices across teams.
  • Facilitate healthy and constructive conflict resolution within the team, encouraging open communication and collaboration.
  • Assist in resource planning and allocation, ensuring the appropriate distribution of team capacity across projects while maintaining balanced workloads and delivery efficiency.
  • Coordinate with management and stakeholders to monitor team capacity, utilization, and project delivery timelines.
  • Perform Jira Management: configure projects, workflows, boards, permissions, and issue types to align with team processes.
  • Manage Jira project settings, sprint boards, and backlogs to ensure accurate and up-to-date project tracking.
  • Conduct regular Jira Audits: Ensure projects are planned in Jira and everyone has tasks, review data integrity, workflow compliance, user access, and ensure reporting accuracy across projects.
  • Generate and publish regular Agile reports: including Sprint Reports, Velocity Charts, Burndown/Burnup Charts, and Cumulative Flow Diagrams — and submit them to the Leadership Team and concerned stakeholders in a timely manner.

Required Qualifications

  • 2–5 years of hands-on experience as a Scrum Master in a software development environment.
  • Strong understanding of Agile methodologies — Scrum and Kanban.
  • Certified Scrum Master (CSM) or Professional Scrum Master (PSM I/II) — added advantage.
  • Proven experience facilitating Scrum events for multiple cross-functional teams.
  • Excellent interpersonal, communication, and stakeholder management skills.
  • Well-versed in Jira — including project configuration, board management, workflow customization, sprint tracking, reporting, and user administration.
  • Strong problem-solving skills with the ability to navigate complex organizational dynamics.
  • Experience working in a fast-paced, iterative software delivery environment.

Preferred Qualifications

  • Advanced Certified Scrum Master (A-CSM) or Professional Scrum Master II (PSM II) certification.
  • Experience in managing and optimizing Kanban workflows for continuous delivery teams.
  • Hands-on experience with Jira Advanced Roadmaps, Jira automation rules, and dashboard creation for leadership reporting.
  • Background or exposure to software development, QA, or product management.
  • Experience in enterprise product companies.

Why You’ll Love Working With Us

  • Meaningful Work with Global Impact: Contribute to enterprise platforms used by world-class organizations such as Philips, Hitachi, Pfizer, and other global leaders—helping them improve how they operate, lead, and grow.
  • People-First, Collaborative Culture: Your ideas are heard, your work is valued, and you’re supported by a team built on trust and respect.
  • Purpose-Driven Technology: Build practical, thoughtful products that solve real operational and leadership challenges.
  • Growth & Ownership: Learn continuously, take ownership of your work, and grow your career in a supportive environment.
  • Global Exposure, Human Connection: Work with diverse teams and enterprise clients worldwide—while feeling connected and supported.
  • Quality, Security & Trust: Our platforms and processes are backed by internationally recognized ISO certifications, reflecting our strong commitment to quality, data security, and operational reliability.

Build a Strong Future Together

At LTS, we don’t just deliver solutions—we build long-term relationships. Together, we create a strong, sustainable future for our people, our clients, and our organization. From continuous learning and career development opportunities to meaningful ownership in your work, we support you at every stage of your journey. Our people benefit from a supportive work environment, competitive benefits, and a culture that values balance, trust, and long-term success.

At LTS, your growth is our growth—and when our people succeed, everything we build becomes stronger.

How to Apply

Interested candidates may forward their detailed resume to careers@ltslean.com with the subject "Application for Scrum Master" and include the following information in the email content:

  • Current CTC:
  • Expected CTC:
  • Notice Period:
  • Current Location:
  • Total Relevant Experience:
  • Willingness to work at office:
summary: A Scrum Master role at Lean Transitions Solutions, requiring 2–5 years of experience, focusing on Agile/Scrum practices, Jira management, and fostering high-performing teams in a global tech environment.
